title: rhodococcum vitis-idaea subsp. minus
intro: Rhodococcum vitis-idaea subsp. minus, also known as Rockcress, is a flowering plant species within the Brassicaceae family. It is a small, hairy perennial plant with white flowers and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting immune function. Rhodococcum vitis-idaea subsp. minus can be used in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
